name: Old-Fashioned Currant Tart
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the butter and sugar with a paddle on medium speed until very soft and light.
2. Add the vanilla and egg yolk, continuing to beat until smooth and shiny (about 3 more minutes).
3. Stop mixing and sift the cake flour into the mixture. Pulse the mixer on and off to incorporate the flour.
4. Scrape the dough onto a piece of plastic wrap, cover, and chill until firm.
5. For the pastry cream:
- Combine milk and half the sugar in a saucepan over low heat.
- Whisk egg yolks with remaining sugar and sifted flour.
- Beat 1/3 of the boiling milk into the yolk mixture, then return the rest to a boil.
- Beat the yolk mixture into the hot milk until thickened and boiled. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla.
- Scrape the cream onto a clean plate, cover with plastic wrap, and chill.
6. Roll the dough and fit it into a 10-to-11-inch tart pan. Line the bottom with parchment paper and fill with dried beans.
7. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
8. Bake the tart shell for about 20 minutes or until set. Remove the parchment and beans, then bake an additional 5 minutes to color the pastry.
9. Do not over-bake. Let cool in the pan.
10. Spread cooled pastry cream onto the tart shell.
11. Arrange fresh red currants on top of the pastry cream.
12. Bring jelly and kirsch to a boil, strain if necessary.
13. Brush glaze from berries.
14. Edge the tart with toasted, sliced almonds and sprinkle with confectioners' sugar.
